page: Amazon Web Services i8ge.48xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by 192 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor architecture running at 2.7 GHz. The system features 1536.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB per core. It includes 120,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps, running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, achieving top 10% results in multi-core stress-ng tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and software compilation. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read and write speeds ranking in the top 10% for both small and large blocks. Database operations and Redis SET operations also place in the top tier, processing 356.82 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the middle 50% average, and image processing benchmarks ranking in the bottom 25%.

The qualitative cost efficiency of this instance is driven by its high resource density, combining massive local NVMe storage capacity with a high-core ARM64 architecture. This profile minimizes the need for external network-attached storage, reducing data transfer overheads. The hardware configuration is optimized for large-scale database deployments, high-throughput static web serving, memory-intensive caching layers, and parallelized data processing tasks that can leverage the 192 dedicated physical cores.
description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.48xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized server powered by 192 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor at 2.7 GHz. It features 1536.0 GB of RAM, 120,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 300 Gbps network bandwidth. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the server delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, as evidenced by its high Redis SET throughput of 356.82 million operations per second. Conversely, single-core benchmarks and image processing tasks represent performance tradeoffs, falling into average and weak tiers respectively. This resource density makes the instance highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring massive local storage and high parallel processing capacity. It is best suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and heavy multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.48xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized server with 192 Graviton physical cores, 1536 GB RAM, and 120,000 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and multi-core performance.

tagline: An ARM64 storage-optimized platform combining massive local NVMe capacity with 192 dedicated cores for high-throughput database and parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 physical cores at 2.7 GHz
120,000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
1536.0 GB of system memory with top-tier read/write bandwidth
300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core, database, and Redis SET performance
Optimized for large-scale databases, web serving, and parallel data processing
